title: Error Handling Mastery
description: Master advanced error handling: catch multiple exception types, use nested try blocks, add context to errors, and implement fallback strategies.
starterCode:
# Exercise: Error Handling Mastery

def multi_except(func, *args):
    """Call func(*args). Return result on success, or a label string for the error type.
    ValueError -> "value_error", TypeError -> "type_error",
    KeyError -> "key_error", other -> "unknown_error"."""
    pass

def nested_try(outer_func, inner_func, data):
    """Try outer_func(inner_func(data)). Handle inner vs outer errors separately.
    Return dict with "source" ("inner" or "outer") and "error" message."""
    pass

def error_context(func, context_msg, *args):
    """Call func(*args). On error, prepend context_msg and re-raise same error type."""
    pass

def with_fallback(primary_func, fallback_func, *args):
    """Try primary_func(*args), if fails try fallback_func(*args), if both fail return None."""
    pass
hints: Order except clauses from most specific to most general
Use nested try/except blocks to distinguish inner from outer errors
type(e) gets the exception class, so you can re-raise with type(e)(new_message)
with_fallback should catch any Exception from primary before trying fallback
Return structured dicts from nested_try so callers know where errors originated
The catch-all except Exception should be last in multi_except

testCases.description: Test with_fallback uses fallback on primary failure
Test nested_try identifies inner error source
